name: Gran Travesía BTT Alt Maestrat Loop
name_original: Tour por el Alto Maestrazgo – Circular desde Vilar de Canes por Culla
description: This difficult 22.3-mile touring cycling route in Alt Maestrat offers challenging climbs, scenic villages, and natural beauty.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Vilar De Canes features a varied terrain with an average altitude of 668 meters, including mountains like the Machorral. The landscape is characterized by terraced fields, almond and olive groves, and holm oaks. Notable features include the challenging Puerto de la Serratella mountain pass and several natural springs. The region also encompasses protected areas such as El Rivet Municipal Nature Park and Barranc dels Horts. Best touring cycling routes around Vilar De Canes The most popular touring cycling route is Gran Travesía BTT Alt Maestrat Loop, a 22.3 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Alt Maestrat region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Coll de la Bandereta – La Torre d'en Doménec loop from Sant Pau, a difficult 35.9 miles (57.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Culla Historic Village – Font d'en Segures loop from Benassal, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Touring cycling around Vilar De Canes is defined by varied terrain, challenging climbs, and natural springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vilar De Canes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Vilar De Canes?
A: There are over a dozen touring cycling routes around Vilar De Canes, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. The region features a diverse terrain with an average altitude of 668 meters, including mountains like the Machorral.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of touring cycling routes in this area?
A: Most touring cycling routes in Vilar De Canes are considered difficult, with significant elevation gains and challenging sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936857584/gran-travesia-btt-alt-maestrat-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gran Travesía BTT Alt Maestrat Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 35.8 km with over 700 meters of ascent. There are also a couple of moderate routes available for those seeking a slightly less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there any easier touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are a few moderate options available. The region's landscape, characterized by terraced fields, almond groves, and holm oaks, offers beautiful scenery even on less demanding sections. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific elevation profiles to find the best fit for your family or skill level.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling around Vilar De Canes?
A: Cyclists can expect a varied and picturesque landscape. The routes often pass through rural areas with small terraced fields, intermingling almond trees, olive groves, and holm oaks. During January and February, the almond blossom transforms the area into a vibrant spectacle of colors. You'll also encounter mountainous terrain and serene natural protected areas.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or landmarks to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You might cycle near or through areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3004757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rivet Municipal Nature Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its centennial oak and holm oak forest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Segures&lt;/a&gt; is a natural spring providing fresh water year-round, perfect for a refreshing stop. The Barranc dels Horts, a Site of Community Interest, also features monumental centenary trees.

Q: Can I find routes that offer challenging climbs and panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging ascents. The "Puerto de la Serratella" is a notable 3rd category mountain pass, stretching almost 14 km with an elevation gain of over 540 meters, offering demanding sections and incredibly rewarding views. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10385032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bandereta – La Torre d'en Doménec loop from Sant Pau&lt;/a&gt;, feature significant elevation gain and panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Vilar De Canes?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Vilar De Canes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20139284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culla Historic Village – Font d'en Segures loop from Benassal&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20139572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Segures – Historic Center of Benassal loop from Benassal&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied rural landscapes and cultural insights.

Q: What are some cultural or historical points of interest I can visit while cycling?
A: Vilar De Canes is situated within the Cultural Park Valltorta-Gassulla, an area of cultural interest. You can incorporate visits to charming medieval villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1113753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culla Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;, with its narrow cobbled streets and castle ruins, into your cycling tours. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6248471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Juan Nepomuceno of La Serratella&lt;/a&gt; is another historical site you might encounter.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vilar De Canes?
A: The region offers beautiful scenery year-round, but for a unique experience, consider visiting during January and February when the almond blossom transforms the landscape into a vibrant spectacle of colors. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, while summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon rides more comfortable.

Q: Are there places to refill water or rest during a ride?
A: Yes, the municipality is known for its natural springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Segures&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural spring that provides fresh water year-round, making it an ideal spot for cyclists to refill bottles and take a break. Several other natural springs are scattered throughout the area, often located in beautiful, vegetated landscapes.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for touring routes in the area?
A: Touring routes in Vilar De Canes vary significantly. Distances can range from around 20 km, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20139303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Benassal loop from Benassal&lt;/a&gt; (20.5 km with 458m ascent), to nearly 60 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10385032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bandereta – La Torre d'en Doménec loop from Sant Pau&lt;/a&gt; (57.8 km with over 1000m ascent). Expect substantial elevation gains, often exceeding 500 meters even on shorter routes.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on a touring cycling route?
A: Yes, the region offers several spots with breathtaking vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1104473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Castle of Ares del Maestre&lt;/a&gt; (Mirador de Ares del Maestrat) provides spectacular panoramic views of the Els Ports region and the surrounding mountainous terrain, perfect for photography and enjoying nature. Many routes also offer rewarding views from their highest points.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring in Vilar De Canes?
A: Over 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vilar De Canes's varied terrain, indicating a popular destination within the cycling community. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the diverse natural landscapes, and the opportunities to discover significant natural protected areas and cultural points of interest.
tips: Typical inland town of the Alto Maestrazgo of Castellón. Cobbled and narrow streets with very colorful and picturesque stone houses!
Fresh water all year round, perfect for filling water and enjoying the place.

Another picturesque and quiet town in the interior of Castellón in Alto Maestrazgo. Its cobbled, narrow streets and its very beautiful stone houses make this town a place that you cannot miss visiting.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: This tour is rated as difficult. The Maestrazgo region is known for its demanding routes with significant elevation changes, including 'savage' and 'really hard' climbs. You'll encounter varied terrain, potentially including single tracks, rocky sections, and loose surfaces. A good level of physical preparation is highly recommended for this ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Expect a challenging and varied terrain. While specific details for this exact route are limited, similar tours in the Maestrazgo region feature single tracks, rocky sections, and loose surfaces. The climbs are often steep, and descents can be quite thrilling, requiring good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this tour in Vilar de Canes?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for Vilar de Canes are not detailed, as a starting point for a circular route, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. Look for designated public parking areas or suitable roadside spots that do not obstruct local traffic.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to Vilar de Canes, the starting point?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Vilar de Canes can be limited, as it's a smaller village in a rural region. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport services in advance, as a car is often the most convenient way to reach the trailhead.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This tour passes by several notable highlights. You'll have the opportunity to see the Historic Center of Benassal, the natural monument Font d'en Segures, and the impressive Parish Church of El Salvador de Culla.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I encounter on this tour?
faqs.answer: The Alto Maestrazgo region offers breathtaking natural landscapes. You'll cycle through deep forests, valleys, and past impressive cliffs. The area is known for its serene and tranquil environments, often referred to as 'empty Spain,' providing a glimpse into rural life and picturesque villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Tour of the Alto Maestrazgo?
faqs.answer: Given the demanding nature and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potential for adverse conditions in higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Alt Maestrat region?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails in the Alt Maestrat region. However, always respect private property, follow local regulations, and be mindful of any signage regarding protected areas or specific land use rules.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of the Alto Maestrazgo – Circular from Vilar de Canes through Culla dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling with dogs can be challenging on difficult routes, if you choose to bring a dog, it's essential to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or in natural areas where wildlife might be present. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and elevation, and always carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, El Camí IT6TR1 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and El Camí IT6TR2 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The tour passes through or near picturesque villages like Culla and Benassal. These villages typically offer cafes, restaurants, and small shops where you can find refreshments, local cuisine, and resupply. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially during off-peak seasons.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of El Salvador de Culla
